title: TxQueue stats not thread-safe despite class docstring warning
description:
File: python/src/lichen/link/tx_queue.py:111-116, 143

The class docstring says:
    '''Thread safety: Not thread-safe. Use external synchronization if
    accessed from multiple async tasks.'''

But TxQueueStats is a plain dataclass with mutable integer fields:

    @dataclass
    class TxQueueStats:
        packets_queued: int = 0
        packets_dropped_deadline: int = 0
        ...

And stats are updated non-atomically:
    self.stats.packets_queued += 1

In Python, += on integers is not atomic. If multiple coroutines access the queue (even with await points between operations), race conditions are possible.

The docstring warning is good, but consider:
1. Using threading.Lock for stats updates
2. Using atomic counters (not available in stdlib, but could use queue)
3. Documenting which operations need external synchronization

P4 since docstring warns about this.
